action: Detect historical continuity queue forks without deleting, rewriting, or collapsing either branch.

selected_from.selection_reason: M-011 preserved automated queue-lineage fork detection as unresolved and proposed a report-only repository inventory. Fork detection is the smallest reusable prerequisite for that inventory.

unresolved_inherited: automatic discovery of undeclared lineage dependencies
semantic matching for differently worded but equivalent unresolved claims

progress.memory: A single current head does not prove a linear history. When two records depend on the same predecessor, the fork remains historical evidence even if a later record reconciles both branches.
progress.verification: Five deterministic checks passed in an isolated Node runtime using byte-identical source and test content.

claim_states.observed: M-011 explicitly retained automated fork detection as unresolved; source, tests, and evidence are committed.
claim_states.inferred: Head-count-only validation can conceal a historical divergence after a merge record restores one current head.
claim_states.proposed: Compose queue-lineage-loader, inherited-unresolved-invariant, and queue-lineage-fork-detector into a report-only inventory over repository M-* records.
claim_states.superseded: Using a single current queue head as sufficient evidence that the queue history is linear.
unresolved: repository-wide discovery of every M-* record
classification of intentional versus accidental forks

privacy_boundary: Public repository metadata, abstract decision structure, and synthetic test records only. Raw private chat, health and pet-health material, credentials, personal identifiers, and unreviewed uploads were excluded.
handoff: Build a report-only repository inventory that discovers all M-* queue records, normalizes them, validates inherited unresolved claims, reports missing dependencies, and preserves every detected fork as evidence.
